vocab.ex: If you want to be a free person who lives the way you want, then you need civil rights.
The students were all united against the teacher's idea for the class trip.
What company does your mum work for? She doesn't work for a company, she works for the government.
Bad things can happen when two sides are full of hate for each other.
We need art in our neighbourhood. Yes, we should have murals on some of the grey walls.
I can't believe they printed a headline with two spelling mistakes in it!
We all have great memories of our school trip.
Soldiers often have to live away from their homes and families for a long time.
The people demanded their civil rights on the streets.
You really shouted at your teacher? I can hardly believe it.
My football team is made up of girls from seven different countries.
There are a lot more brick houses in northern Germany than in the south.
You must link your sentences together with words like 'but', 'and' or 'so'.
Some people hang their country's flag in front of their houses on special days.
The little girl is banging her cup on her plate and it's getting on my nerves.
Who is banging that drum so late at night? I can't sleep!
You're too loud! Please shut the door, I'm trying to do my homework.
Everybody's here, except Jenny.
Who were the men who broke the church windows? They weren't men, they were youths, all about 15 years old.
I didn't recognise him. He was wearing a costume.
I don't walk down that street at night; there aren't any streetlights, so it's scary.
I don't know that man – he's a stranger.
We need new pots for our plants.
We've got a lot of extra paint, so let's paint your room with it.
Be careful with your glass; it'll tip over and your laptop will get wet.
Those people are sleeping on the pavement. That makes me sad.
Be careful, the floor is wet, so you might slip!
You're a great runner; you should try the 100 metres!
You need speed to win the race; you're too slow.
Many Catholics go to Rome to see the pope on St Peter's Square.
Oh no, that girl forgot her bag! Yes, it's hers, I'll go give it to her tomorrow.
You scared me! I didn't hear your footsteps and when I turned around you were right in front of me!
Your teacher called and told me you beat somebody up. What's wrong with you?
I don't like fast running, I like to jog.
How's Andy? He's angry at me: We were arguing, and then he just walked off and hasn't come back.
Your presentation had great photos of Dublin, but there wasn't enough factual information about the city and its history.
Excuse me, but this coach is a quiet zone. You can't have loud phone conversations here.
Never leave your rubbish behind after a picnic.
What I did was wrong, so I must apologise. That's OK, we all make mistakes.
Your teenage years are the time from age 13 to 19.
I know she stole the phone, but she won't admit it.
In the UK, people call children in primary school pupils.
Listen everyone! We're going to go to the Victoria & Albert Museum for our field trip to London.
Cookbooks are great for all the nice photos, but when I cook, I like to use online recipes on my tablet.
I'm not patient enough to read instruction manuals. That's why you always get angry when you realise you've done something wrong.
Getting sunburnt on my first day of a holiday isn't my idea of a good time.
Can we stop cycling and take a break? My thighs hurt!
I'd like to work in a hospital because I'd like to help sick people.
I've got a really bad pain in my left foot.
Don't stay in the sun too long, your skin is already getting red!

vocab.en: to be unable to do sth
vocab.de: unfähig sein, etw. zu tun; nicht in der Lage sein, etw. zu tun
vocab.ex: On nice, sunny days, I'm unable to focus on my homework.
Our town is facing a lot of problems, but things will be OK if we all work together.
The newspaper reported on a poor little boy who got badly sunburnt.
Your skin never forgets sunburns, so it's best to avoid them.
I hate it when commenters post really rude comments.
If you don't use sunscreen, you'll get a sunburn.
When I go to the gym, I need to wear a tank top so my arms are free.
You're red as a lobster, what a terrible sunburn!
When I saw London for the first time, it really affected me in a good way: I knew I wanted to live there!
You needn't read the pages with a red 'x' across the page.
Lauren always says, 'A good education can open a lot of doors in life, so I try to get good marks at school.'
Make sure you're wearing swimming things under your clothes; swimming is a part of gorge walking on this trip!

vocab.de: auf Entdeckungsreise gehen; sich umschauen; erkunden; erforschen
vocab.ex: A new park? Great! Let's go and explore!
I have to do my homework now. Otherwise I won't be able to go shopping later.
The Covid-19 pandemic was the biggest one since the pandemic in 1918.
The storm last night did a lot of damage.
I'm going to go to a football camp at the weekend. Oh no, you'll miss out on my party!
We shouldn't walk on this part of the mountain, there are no paths.
For the next part of our radio show, we're going to talk to an expert about how much sun is dangerous for different skin types.
I'll send you a postcard from New York. A postcard?! No one sends postcards any more.
From far away, our planet looks like a big blue ball.
For me a film is really good if you can relate to the characters.
We have a maths test tomorrow. Yuk, don't remind me.
You aren't allowed to look at your phone while you're cycling, so stop it. You'll crash into somebody!
We aren't in a hurry, so let's leave the motorway and drive through the countryside.
We're on our way to the airport, but we're late: I hope we can still catch our plane!
The trip will be expensive, but the price includes all our meals.
Don't bring too much on your trip to England; bring only what is necessary.
